schema_note: Sidecar overlay — NOT part of the deterministic extractor output (pack.json). pack.json is regenerated from the manual and must never be hand-edited; human-confirmed application context lives here until Run C ships the confirmation-overlay merge + `technician_confirmed` provenance tier (see tools/drive-pack-extract/RUN_C_PLAN.md item 3). Grading (grading/domain_rules.py) does not read this file today; its provenance vocabulary is still {bench_verified, manual_cited}.

confirmations.statement: In a crane application the meaning of the LL1/LL2 (Lower Limit) inputs depends on which motion the IMPULSE drive controls. On a HOIST drive, LL1/LL2 are usually the hoist lower limit (LL1 = slow-down as the hook block approaches the lower limit, LL2 = stop at the lower limit). On a TRAVERSE drive (trolley or bridge), the same inputs serve as travel (end-of-travel) limits — LL1 slow-down, LL2 stop.

manual_grounding.doc: Magnetek IMPULSE G+ Mini Technical Manual (144-25085)
manual_grounding.excerpt: LL1 Lower Limit 1—SLOW DOWN Indicator. Lower 1. May not require corrective action.

manual_grounding.doc: Magnetek IMPULSE G+ Mini Technical Manual (144-25085)
manual_grounding.excerpt: LL2 Lower Limit 2—STOP Indicator. Lower Limit 1. May not require corrective action.

manual_grounding.doc: Magnetek IMPULSE G+ Mini Technical Manual (144-25085)

consumption_notes: LL1/LL2 are limit-switch INPUT indications (the manual: 'May not require corrective action'), not drive hardware faults — the indication means the axis reached its slow-down/stop limit switch on input terminal H01.XX (see U01.10).
Any MIRA/Drive Commander answer about LL1/LL2 on this family MUST first establish which motion the drive controls (hoist vs traverse) before naming the limit, or present both readings.

SAFETY: limit switches are a protective function. Never advise bypassing, jumpering, or defeating a limit input to clear the indication. Corrective guidance stays within the manual's cited actions: check limit-switch position, condition, and the H01.XX input terminal configuration.

confirmations.statement: PROPOSED by symmetry with the confirmed LL1/LL2 semantics, NOT technician-confirmed: UL* (Upper Limit) inputs would read as the hoist UPPER limit on a hoist drive (safety-critical: upper-limit protection against two-blocking) and as the opposite-direction travel limit on a traverse drive. Requires explicit technician confirmation before any answer surface asserts it.
consumption_notes: Do NOT auto-promote: proposed → confirmed requires an explicit technician/admin sign-off (same doctrine as kg proposed→verified).

If confirmed for a hoist axis, the upper limit is the more safety-critical direction (block over-travel / two-blocking); the answer judge in Run C item 4 should treat UL* with the same never-bypass rule as LL*.
